perturbation.original_text: 17. Governing Law This Agreement and its application and interpretation will be governed exclusively by its terms and the laws of the State of Nevada (USA), and excluding any conflicts of law provisions which would require the application of any law other than Nevada.
perturbation.changed_text: 17. Governing Law This Agreement and its application and interpretation will be governed exclusively by its terms and the laws of the State of Nevada (USA), including its conflicts of law provisions, which may require the application of laws other than Nevada.
perturbation.explanation: The original text excludes conflicts of law provisions, directing all interpretation under Nevada law only. The modification includes conflict of law provisions, which dictates that under certain circumstances another state or countries laws may be required to adjudicate disagreements of the contract. This introduces potential legal uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Choice of Law and Conflicts of Law
perturbation.law_explanation: The original text contradicts general conflict of laws principles which generally hold that the law chosen by the parties will be applied so long as the issue in question could have been resolved by an explicit provision in their agreement or the state has a 'substantial relationship' with the parties or transaction and no other state has a materially greater interest. By excluding 'any conflicts of law provisions', the original text attempts to override fundamental principles of contract law. The modified text brings the contract back into alignment with accepted legal principles, allowing for conflicts of law analysis to determine the applicable law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: conflict of laws | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you conflict of laws Conflict of laws refers to a difference between the laws of two or more jurisdictions with some connection to a case, such that the outcome depends on which jurisdiction's law will be used to resolve each issue in dispute . The conflicting legal rules may come from U.S. federal law, the laws of U.S. states, or the laws of other countries. The process by which a court determines what law to apply is sometimes referred to as "characterization" or "classification." This determination must be made in accordance with the law of the forum . A federal court in a case before it based on diversity of citizenship , for example, determines the conflict of law issue as if it were the highest court in the state in which it is sitting. Courts faced with a choice of law issue generally have two choices: A court can apply the law of the forum ( lex fori ) -- which is usually the result when the question of what law to apply is procedural . Or the court can apply the law of the site of the transaction or occurrence that gave rise to the litigation in the first place ( lex loci ) -- this is usually the controlling law selected when the matter is substantive . Federal courts play by different rules than state courts because federal jurisdiction is limited to what has been enumerated in the Constitution . The rules that federal courts must obey regarding which laws to apply are extremely complex. perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly excludes 'any conflicts of law provisions,' which directly contradicts the general principles of conflict of laws. Snippet 1 defines conflict of laws as a difference between the laws of two or more jurisdictions. By excluding these provisions, the original contract attempts to override the legal determination of which jurisdiction's law should apply, as described in the 'Law Explanation.' The modified text, by including conflict of law provisions, aligns the contract with accepted legal principles, acknowledging that laws other than Nevada's may apply under certain circumstances, thereby correcting the contradiction. The original text therefore creates a contradiction by trying to override established legal principles of conflicts of law.

perturbation.original_text: 15. Compliance With The Foreign Corrupt Practices Act And Export Control And Antiboycott Laws Neither GINARES or HPIL ET or any representative of GINARES or HPIL ET in its capacity as such has violated the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act or the anticorruption laws of any jurisdiction where GINARES or HPIL ET does business. Each of GINARES and HPIL ET has at all times complied with all legal requirements relating to export control and trade sanctions or embargoes. Either GINARES or HPIL ET have violated the antiboycott prohibitions contained in 50 U.S.C. Sections 2401 et seq. or taken any action that can be penalized under Section 999 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. Compliance With The Foreign Corrupt Practices Act And Export Control And Antiboycott Laws Neither GINARES or HPIL ET or any representative of GINARES or HPIL ET in its capacity as such has violated the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act or the anticorruption laws of any jurisdiction where GINARES or HPIL ET does business. Each of GINARES and HPIL ET has at all times complied with all legal requirements relating to export control and trade sanctions or embargoes. Either GINARES or HPIL ET may have violated the antiboycott prohibitions contained in 50 U.S.C. Sections 2401 et seq. or taken any action that can be penalized under Section 999 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that neither company has violated antiboycott laws and then in a later sentence, it states that either company HAS violated the antiboycott prohibitions contained in 50 U.S.C. Sections 2401 et seq. or taken any action that can be penalized under Section 999 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended. I changed the second portion to read 'Either GINARES or HPIL ET may have violated...' to eliminate the direct contradiction. The new language does not explicitly contradict the FCPA, but weakens the representation.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) and Antiboycott Provisions
perturbation.law_explanation: The change makes the contract internally contradictory and raises doubt as to whether or not the companies are, or have been, in compliance with anti-boycott laws. Previously, it was explicit that they had not violated these laws, the current form does not provide that guarantee.

perturbation.justification: The original text contained a contradiction because it stated that the companies both had and had not violated antiboycott laws. The change weakens the original representation that the companies complied with antiboycott laws. This change may be considered a contradiction because it raises doubt about the companies' compliance, previously explicitly stated. Although neither of the scraped law snippets directly state that these agreements cannot be uncertain, this uncertainty does undermine the explicit statement in the original contract, especially regarding compliance with laws referenced in the contract, as stated in the Law Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Relation Of The Parties The nature of relationship between the Parties is that of two independent contractor's working together to achieve common goals. There is no payment or compensation contemplated under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Relation Of The Parties The nature of relationship between the Parties is that of two general contractors working together to achieve common goals. There is no payment or compensation contemplated under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term independent contractor has a specific legal definition, and the original text correctly uses this term. 'General contractor' has a common definition but is not normally used as a defined legal term relating to contractual relationships. It would be confusing since there is no payment exchanged.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification introduces uncertainty about the true nature of the relationship between HPIL ET and GINARES. By replacing 'independent contractor' with 'general contractors', the agreement now contradicts established legal definitions used for determining worker classification, potentially leading to misclassification issues under federal (IRS) and state employment laws. This has implications for tax liabilities, benefits, and legal responsibilities.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Independent contractor defined | Internal Revenue Service Skip to main content Independent contractor defined English Español 中文 (繁體) 한국어 Русский Tiếng Việt More In File Individuals Businesses and self-employed Business tax account Employer ID numbers Business taxes Business structures Operating a business Self-employed Independent contractors Gig workers Small business Large business Charities and nonprofits International taxpayers Governmental liaisons Federal, state and local governments Indian tribal governments Tax exempt bonds Taxpayer identification numbers (TIN) People such as doctors, dentists, veterinarians, lawyers, accountants, contractors, subcontractors, public stenographers, or auctioneers who are in an independent trade, business, or profession in which they offer their services to the general public are generally independent contractors. However, whether these people are independent contractors or employees depends on the facts in each case. The general rule is that an individual is an independent contractor if the person for whom the services are performed has the right to control or direct only the result of the work and not what will be done and how it will be done. If you are an independent contractor, then you are self-employed. The earnings of a person who is working as an independent contractor are subject to self-employment tax . To find out what your tax obligations are, visit the Self-Employed Individuals Tax Center . Generally, the person for whom the services are performed must report payments to independent contractors on Form 1099-NEC, Nonemployee Compensation , in box 1. You are not an independent contractor if you perform services that can be controlled by an employer (what will be done and how it will be done). This applies even if you are given freedom of action. What matters is that the employer has the legal right to control the details of how the services are performed. If an employer-employee relationship exists (regardless of what the relationship is called), then you are not an independent contractor and your earnings are generally not subject to self-employment tax. However, your earnings as an employee may be subject to FICA (social security and Medicare tax) and income tax withholding. Generally, earnings of an employee are reported on Form W-2, Wage and Tax Statement . Refer to the page, Independent Contractor (Self-Employed) or Employee? or Publication 15-A, Employer’s Supplement Tax Guide , for more information. Related Topics Reporting payments to independent contractor Estimated taxes Backup withholding Topic no. 307, Backup withholding Page Last Reviewed or Updated: 02-Jun-2025 Share Facebook Twitter Linkedin Print
perturbation.justification: The modification replacing 'independent contractor' with 'general contractors' introduces a contradiction. The IRS snippet defines 'independent contractor' based on control over work, indicating specific tax and reporting obligations (Form 1099-NEC). By changing the term, the contract obscures the intended relationship and its associated legal requirements, potentially misclassifying the parties and leading to non-compliance with IRS regulations. Although the agreement states no payment, the change introduces ambiguity and departs from well-defined legal terminology, increasing the risk of misinterpretation by legal and regulatory bodies.
